pi_items.source: Consistent across setup_task.sh (line 10), export_result.sh (line 12), and task.json metadata. The path is where the PNG output is expected to be saved.
verifier.py line 74 enforces: width > 400 (minimum 401 pixels). Metadata claims minimum of 400, and verifier checks strictly > 400.
verifier.py line 74 enforces: height > 400 (minimum 401 pixels). Metadata claims minimum of 400, and verifier checks strictly > 400.

verifier.py lines 82-93 check background color by sampling 10x10 patches from all four corners. Pixels must have mean >= 250 to pass as white (allowing for JPEG compression artifacts). Pure white [255, 255, 255] satisfies this threshold. Task description (line 3 of task.json) explicitly requires 'Set the background color to **Solid White**'.

verifier.py lines 101-115 verify red color by filtering non-white pixels and checking that R > G+30 and R > B+30. This confirms red dominance. Task description (line 3 of task.json) explicitly requires 'Set the 3D surface color to **Red**'. Pure red [255, 0, 0] satisfies the red dominance check.

setup_invesalius.sh downloads the official InVesalius CT Cranium dataset from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip and stores it in /home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium (via symlink). This is the publicly documented sample dataset from InVesalius official download page (https://invesalius.github.io/download.html) listed as 'Cranium' dataset. The series is a real CT scan of the human skull (cranium).

privileged_info_summary: This task uses the official InVesalius 0051 CT Cranium dataset, a real publicly available medical imaging sample from the InVesalius v3.0 release (https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip). The task requires generating a 3D surface visualization of the skull from the CT scan with specific visual properties: red color for the 3D object, white solid background, minimum dimensions of 400x400 pixels, anterior (frontal) camera view, and clean interface (no bounding box/axes visible). The output must be exported as a PNG image to /home/ga/Documents/illustration_base.png. All metadata property claims (output path, dimensions, background color, object color, and dataset name) are verified against task setup scripts and the verifier implementation.
